_source: Offline fixture — no PDF extraction or OCR
objective: Work together to chart a safe path through an asteroid field by placing navigation tiles. Connect your ship to the jump gate before the drift timer runs out.
setup: Place the drift timer track in view of all players and set the timer marker on space 12.
Shuffle all 48 navigation tiles face-down into a draw stack.
Place the ship miniature on the starting edge of the play area.
Place the jump gate tile 5 tile-widths away from the ship in a straight line.
Scatter 10 asteroid tokens randomly in the space between ship and jump gate.
Deal 4 fuel cards to each player; place the rest face-down as the fuel deck.

turnStructure.description: On your turn: Draw one navigation tile from the stack. Then Place it adjacent to any existing tile, connecting path edges. Tiles cannot overlap asteroids. Finally, Drift: advance the timer marker one space. If the timer reaches zero before the path is complete, the crew loses.
turnStructure.endCondition: The game ends immediately when a continuous path of navigation tiles connects the ship to the jump gate (win) or when the drift timer reaches zero (loss).

coreMechanic.name: Cooperative Tile Placement
coreMechanic.description: Players must coordinate tile placement to build a connected route around asteroids. Each tile has path connectors on its edges; only matching connectors can be placed adjacent. Fuel cards let players perform special actions like rotating a tile already placed or removing one asteroid token.

scoring.description: Stellar Drift is a cooperative pass/fail game. If the path connects ship to jump gate, all players win together. Remaining timer spaces and fuel cards determine a bonus star rating from 1 to 5 stars.
scoring.winCondition: All players win if the ship-to-gate path is completed before the drift timer reaches zero.

edgeCases: If the navigation tile stack runs out before the path is complete, shuffle all discarded fuel cards to form a smaller emergency tile stack of 5 tiles.
If no legal placement exists for a drawn tile, the player must discard it and advance the drift timer one additional space.
Fuel cards that remove asteroids can only target tokens not adjacent to already-placed tiles.
In solo mode, the player draws 2 tiles per turn but the drift timer advances by 2 each Drift phase.
